title: Hypertension
subtitle: Topic Journey
description: Move through the topic like a guided learning quest. Complete each stop to unlock the next one.

nodes.title: Start
bullets: Follow the path step by step
Complete each stop to unlock the next one
Reveal the full map at the end
nodes.memoryHint: This journey is about sequence and big-picture recall.

nodes.title: Big Picture
nodes.summary: Hypertension is common, often silent, and dangerous when uncontrolled.
bullets: Affects a large number of adults
Raises risk of heart disease, stroke, and kidney disease

nodes.title: Pharmacist Role
nodes.summary: Pharmacists help with screening, monitoring, counseling, and adherence.
nodes.memoryHint: Pharmacists improve follow-up and treatment success.

nodes.title: Primary vs Secondary
nodes.summary: Most hypertension is primary; secondary hypertension has an identifiable cause.
bullets: Secondary: due to renal disease, adrenal disease, OSA, or drugs
nodes.memoryHint: Primary = majority. Secondary = find the cause.

nodes.title: Risk Factors
nodes.summary: Primary hypertension often develops in the presence of multiple risk factors.

nodes.title: Pathophysiology Boss
nodes.summary: Hypertension is driven mainly by SNS and RAAS activation.
bullets: These pathways raise vascular tone and/or volume

nodes.title: RAAS Route
nodes.summary: RAAS raises blood pressure through sodium retention and vasoconstriction.
bullets: Aldosterone increases sodium and water reabsorption
nodes.memoryHint: RAAS mainly increases volume and vascular tone.

nodes.title: SNS Route
nodes.summary: SNS raises blood pressure through vessel constriction and heart stimulation.
bullets: Beta-1 stimulation → higher heart rate and contractility
Higher SVR and cardiac output raise BP

nodes.title: Core Formula
nodes.summary: Blood pressure depends on vascular resistance and cardiac output.

nodes.title: Diagnosis Gate
nodes.summary: Diagnosis requires repeated readings, not a single office value.
bullets: Use an average of at least 2 readings
Readings should be obtained on at least 2 occasions
White coat hypertension can mislead decisions

nodes.title: BP Classification
nodes.summary: Blood pressure is classified into four major categories.

nodes.title: Lifestyle Management
nodes.summary: Lifestyle changes are essential in both prevention and treatment.
nodes.memoryHint: Lifestyle is always part of HTN management.

nodes.title: Final Pearl
nodes.summary: Natural products are not guideline-recommended for BP lowering.
nodes.memoryHint: Natural does not always mean harmless.

nodes.title: Journey Complete
bullets: You now have the full sequence in mind
Open the full map and review any stop again
Next step: connect this journey to question practice
nodes.memoryHint: Review the map once more to lock the sequence in memory.
